title: Ecbolium collinum
taxonomy: Ecbolium collinum is classified within the plant family Acanthaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus family. The Acanthaceae family is part of the order Lamiales, which includes plants with bilaterally symmetrical flowers and four- or five-merous corollas. The Lamiales order is further classified within the clade asterids, one of the two main clades that make up the angiosperms or flowering plants. The asterids clade is also known as the "flowering stem plants" and includes plants with a wide range of growth habits and morphologies. Overall, Ecbolium collinum's classification reflects its relationships with other flowering plants and its position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Ecbolium collinum, also known as "Devil's Claw's cousin", is a flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, muscle and joint pain, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ecbolium collinum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to support joint health and reduce inflammation.
